title: Fixed-point Representations: A+S, C1, C2
summary: Compares sign-magnitude, one's complement, and two's complement fixed-point representations: ranges, precision, negation algorithms, and arithmetic.
content_points: A+S: leftmost bit = sign; magnitude in remaining bits. Two representations for 0; range ±(2^(n-1) - 2^(-m)).
C1: negate by inverting all bits. Also two zeros; same range as A+S. Addition needs end-around carry.
C2: negate by inverting all bits and adding 1. Single zero; asymmetric range [-2^(n-1), 2^(n-1)-2^(-m)]. Addition identical to unsigned – one adder works for both.
C2 is the universally used integer representation in modern processors.
learning_objectives: Describe and compare A+S, C1, C2 representations
Perform negation in C1 and C2
Explain why C2 is preferred for integer arithmetic
